findings.evidence: `rg` confirms `Gradings.lean:165` has `G.swapMarkings.maslovO x = G.maslovX x`, `Gradings.lean:171` has `G.swapMarkings.maslovX x = G.maslovO x`, and `Gradings.lean:179-180` has `G.swapMarkings.alexander x = -G.alexander x - (((n : ℤ) - 1 : ℤ) : ℚ)`; integer versions are at `GradingInteger.lean:180`, `186`, and `193-194`.
findings.fix: Rewrite this sentence to say transpose preserves the gradings, while marking swap exchanges `M_O`/`M_X` and sends Alexander to the documented shifted negative.
findings.issue: The docstring says the diagonal reflection and `O`/`X` marking swap both leave Maslov and Alexander gradings unchanged; marking swap does not preserve Alexander grading and only exchanges the two Maslov gradings.

summary: The new rectangle-count and differential statements are semantically faithful, but the module documentation makes a false grading claim about marking swap.
